title: Zendesk Trigger webhook accepts unsigned requests (missing HMAC-SHA256 signature verification)
description: The ZendeskTrigger node's webhook() handler reads the incoming HTTP request body and immediately returns it as the workflow trigger payload, without verifying the HMAC-SHA256 signature that Zendesk attaches to every outbound webhook. Any party that knows (or guesses) the workflow's webhook URL can therefore send a forged, unsigned POST request and start the workflow with attacker-controlled data, impersonating Zendesk. This is an authentication-bypass-by-spoofing (CWE-290): the node trusts the request origin without authenticating it.
